Death on the Nile (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,630,104Details
International Box Office $91,677,131
Worldwide Box Office $137,307,235
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $469,064
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $764,860
Total $1,233,924
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Belgian sleuth Hercule Poirot’s Egyptian vacation aboard a glamorous river steamer turns into a terrifying search for a murderer when a picture-perfect couple’s idyllic honeymoon is tragically cut short. Set against a landscape of sweeping desert vistas and the Giza pyramids, this tale of unbridled passion and incapacitating jealousy features a cosmopolitan group of impeccably dressed travelers and enough wicked twists and turns to keep audiences guessing until the final, shocking denouement.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,891,123 (28.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$90,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,280 opening theaters/3,420 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $51,826,782

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 11th, 2022 (Wide) by 20th Century Studios
International Releases: February 9th, 2022 (Wide), released as Tod auf dem Nil (Switzerland)
February 9th, 2022 (Wide) (Philippines)
February 9th, 2022 (Wide) (Belgium)
February 9th, 2022 (Wide) (Indonesia)
February 9th, 2022 (Wide) (Netherlands)
Video Release: March 29th, 2022 by 20th Century Studios
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence, some bloody images, and sexual material.
(Rating bulletin 2713 (cert #52577), 1/5/2022)
Running Time: 127 minutes
Franchise: Kenneth Branagh's Hercule Poirot
Filming location (country): England, United Kingdom
Plot point: Murder Mystery
Subgenre: Mystery Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 20th Century Studios, Kinberg Genre, The Mark Gordon Company, Scott Free Films, TSG Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Belgian sleuth Hercule Poirot’s Egyptian vacation aboard a glamorous river steamer turns into a terrifying search for a murderer when a picture-perfect couple’s idyllic honeymoon is tragically cut short. Set against a landscape of sweeping desert vistas and the Giza pyramids, this tale of unbridled passion and incapacitating jealousy features a cosmopolitan group of impeccably dressed travelers and enough wicked twists and turns to keep audiences guessing until the final, shocking denouement.
